Ecosystem

The Faber ecosystem includes a standard library (Norma), a graphics and geometry library (Triga), a utility library (coreutils), reader locale packages, and a growing corpus of examples.

Norma standard library

Core modules for I/O, collections, strings, math, and system interaction. Read more →

Triga graphics and geometry

Vector math, matrices, transforms, and GPU-friendly geometric types. Read more →

Coreutils

Everyday utility functions built on Norma. Demonstrates idiomatic Faber. Read more →

Reader locale packages

TOML packs that map Faber keywords, types, and diagnostics to natural language surfaces. Read more →

AI workbench

Tooling for LLM-assisted Faber development, including agent skills and the diagnostics contract. Read more →

Language corpus

The example corpus — 154 directories, 292 .fab files covering every keyword, operator, and type. The source of truth for generated pages. Read more →
